title: Christmas Tree Tray
summary: A stepped, layered wood tray shaped like a Christmas tree — every row is a different compound angle, mounted on a plywood backer.
description:
Each visible "branch" layer is its own short piece of 1x2, cut at two different angles on its two ends — none of them square, and none of them matching each other — which is what produces the tapering tree silhouette once they're all stacked and offset by a consistent 1/2" per row. Getting the top pieces right matters most, since the source specifically calls out that the top angles differ from the angles used lower down; treat each row's cut list note as its own small puzzle rather than assuming the pattern repeats exactly.

Build bottom to top, checking the offset and angle direction of each new row against the one below it before nailing — a single reversed angle low in the stack throws off every row above it. The plywood backer is cut to match the finished tree's outline only after the tree itself is built, using the assembled shape as its own template.

tools.note: Traces and cuts the plywood backer to match the finished tree shape.
Every 1x2 piece needs a precise compound angle — check each row's angle direction against the cut list before cutting.

materials.name: Pine, 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Furring strip works as a cheaper substitute.

cutList.note: One at 12-3/8", one at 11-3/8" — long point to long point, one end at 40° off square, the other at 25° off square, ends not parallel.
Long point to short point, ends parallel, both ends cut at 25° off square.
Long point to long point, ends not parallel, both ends cut at 25° off square.
Trace from the assembled tree shape before cutting.

steps.title: Cut and attach the top pieces
steps.body: Carefully cut the two top pieces, noting that their angles are different from the angles used lower in the tree. Attach together with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Layer the next rows
steps.body: Layer each subsequent row 1/2" below and outside the one above it, attaching with glue and nails. Repeat this pattern down the tree, checking the angle direction on each new piece against the cut list before nailing.

steps.title: Attach the base
steps.body: Build the base piece and end pieces, then attach the completed base to the bottom of the tree with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Cut and attach the backer
steps.body: Lay the assembled tree shape on the 1/4" plywood and trace its outline. Cut along the line with a jigsaw, sand any rough edges, and attach the backer to the back of the tree with 3/4" brad nails and glue.
